export_control_resources.name: FR 2025-19846 — BIS Affiliates Rule Suspension (Primary Source)
export_control_resources.description: Primary Federal Register notice (90 FR 47201) suspending the Affiliates Rule expansion for one year. Governs Phase 1 (10 Nov 2025 – 9 Nov 2026) and specifies automatic Phase 2 reimposition on 10 Nov 2026.

export_control_resources.name: BIS Adopts 50 Percent Rule for Export Controls
export_control_resources.description: Analysis of BIS adoption of the OFAC-style 50 Percent Rule, key divergences from OFAC (no aggregation; foreign-only scope), and compliance program implications.

export_control_resources.name: BIS Expands Impact of U.S. Export Controls with 50 Percent Rule
export_control_resources.description: Operational impact of the Affiliates Rule on counterparty screening workflows, beneficial-ownership documentation requirements, and integration with existing OFAC SDN programs.

export_control_resources.name: DOC Suspends Enforcement of the Affiliates Rule for One Year
export_control_resources.description: Enforcement-edge analysis of the suspension alongside bundled China Entity List removals; covers conditionality and US-China trade-framework context.

export_control_resources.name: Trade Compliance Flash: BIS Issues Long-Awaited Affiliates Rule
export_control_resources.description: Covers EAR licence exception eligibility, MEU List vs. Entity List treatment under the 50 Percent Rule, and interim compliance steps during the Phase 1 suspension window.

upcoming_deadlines.title: BIS Affiliates Rule — Phase 2 Snap-Back Takes Effect
upcoming_deadlines.description: Phase 1 (10 Nov 2025 – 9 Nov 2026): one-year suspension of the BIS Affiliates Rule expansion (EAR §744.21). No EAR authorisation needed for controlled-technology sharing with foreign affiliates ≥50% owned by Entity List / MEU List designees during this window. Phase 2 (from 10 Nov 2026, indefinite): full controls automatically reimposed unless BIS publishes a further Federal Register extension before that date. The suspension does NOT require companies to delay compliance preparation — due-diligence infrastructure must be in place before Phase 2.
upcoming_deadlines.authority: U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS)
upcoming_deadlines.action_required: By 9 Nov 2026: (1) map all foreign-affiliate ownership chains to the ≥50% threshold for every Entity List and MEU List designee in your supply chain; (2) implement or update Technology Control Plans; (3) ensure EAR licence authorisations are on file for any controlled items shared with in-scope affiliates; (4) monitor Federal Register for any extension notice — none may be assumed.

compliance_note: Export controls are enforced across multiple jurisdictions. Monitor BIS (US), UAE IEC (Emirates), DOJ (enforcement), and international regimes (Wassenaar) for changing restrictions on technology, equipment, and services.
